column1: Refactor scopes for Servlet API jar,RESTEASY-2041,12774558,Bug,Resolved,RESTEASY,RESTEasy,software,ron_sigal,,http://jboss.org/resteasy,Major,Done,ron_sigal,ron_sigal,ron_sigal,15/Oct/18 8:31 PM,11/Mar/19 4:28 AM,29/Oct/19 12:21 PM,11/Mar/19 4:28 AM,4.0.0.Beta5,,4.0.0.CR1,,,,,,0,WildFlyUnrelated,"When I added a reference to ServletContext in resteasy-core, a test in resteasy-rxjava failed because it couldn't find ServletContext. That's because the test doesn't run in Wildfly, so jboss-servlet-api_3.1_spec doesn't get provided. We're saying that resteasy-rxjava needs to have an explicit dependency on jboss-servlet-api_3.1_spec, even though it doesn't directly reference anything in jboss-servlet-api_3.1_spec.

For RESTEasy applications running in Wildfly, jboss-servlet-api_3.1_spec is ""provided"" by the servlet container. For a standalone program using, say, resteasy-netty4, NettyJaxrsServer is serving as an embedded servlet container, and, by analogy, it should supply jboss-servlet-api_3.1_spec. That is, it should have a dependency on jboss-servlet-api_3.1_spec with scope ""compile"".
\[Note that resteasy-undertow needs the servlet api to compile.\] Adding
to resteasy-netty, resteasy-netty4, and resteasy-netty4-cdi, mvn dependency:tree gives

Rules activation not canceled in Case Management subprocess,JBPM-8249,12792558,Bug,Resolved,JBPM,jBPM,software,swiderski.maciej,"jBPM is a flexible business process management suite.

,http://jboss.org/jbpm,Major,Done,swiderski.maciej,bvanderwalt,bvanderwalt,15/Feb/19 11:08 AM,11/Mar/19 6:07 AM,29/Oct/19 12:21 PM,11/Mar/19 6:07 AM,7.16.0.Final,7.17.0.Final,7.19.0.Final,,KieServer,,,,0,,Rules activation not canceled in Case Management subprocess.
I have the scenario where two objects of type GeneralRiskRating are inserted into the rules engine by one of many rules that looks like this (see file smoking_risk.gdst):

These two objects trigger the activation of another rule (name: ""Create new General RiskRatingRollup if not exist"" in file: rollup_risk_ratings_insured.drl ) in a different agenda group (the next rule task in the process flow):
rule ""Create new General RiskRatingRollup if not exist""
The rule is activated to fire for each of the two newly inserted GeneralRiskRating objects,

but the condition of the rule (condition: not RiskRatingRollup(riskType == $risk.riskType ) ) is suppose to cancel the activation of the rule for the second object after it fired once for the first GeneralRiskRating object.
The problem is that the rule ""Create new General RiskRatingRollup if not exist"" is firing two (2) times instead of one (1) time, thus not canceling the second activation of the rule.

If the rules are executed outside of a bpm workflow it works fine. If the milestone in the process flow is replaces with a new exact copy the rules fire correctly (thus canceling the second activations)
To reproduce the issue all that is needed is to create a new case by using the rest interface:
just replace the ""pam_user_id"" with your username you use to log into jBPM. Also make sure to use the same username/pw for the basic auth in the HTTP request.
When the request is made, it will start the process (application_process.bpmn2) and all the rules will fire in the subprocess (calculate_questionnaire_risk).
In the server log you will observe the following log statement TWO times instead of just once:

If the process diagram is reduced to only the rules and no milestone things start to work correctly again. It appears that this given setup (see attached zip file) will allow you to reproduce the issue and hopefully you should be able to identify the issue.

(7.2.z) RESTEASY-2148 - Add the ability to disable Filename encoding in Content-Disposition,JBEAP-16427,12793415,Bug,Verified,JBEAP,JBoss Enterprise Application Platform,software,tomjenkinson,"JBoss Enterprise Application Platform:

Cloud-ready, market-leading enterprise platform for next-generation, highly transactional enterprise Java applications",http://www.redhat.com/products/jbossenterprisemiddleware/application-platform/,Critical,Done,spyrkob,bmaxwell,bmaxwell,20/Feb/19 10:27 AM,29/May/19 10:30 AM,29/Oct/19 12:21 PM,11/Mar/19 5:35 AM,,,7.2.2.CR1,7.2.2.GA,REST,,,,0,regression,"A change was introduced for RESTEASY-2046 to encode non-US-ASCII filenames per RFC 5987, but the fix was incorrect/incomplete.
Also latest RFC for multipart messages inside HTTP is defined in [RFC-7578|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7578#section-4.2] and there is stated:
* NOTE: The encoding method described in RFC5987, which would add a ""filename*"" parameter to the Content-Disposition header field, MUST NOT be used.
Commons FileUpload library cannot consume file correctly if filename is like *filename\*=utf-8''Test%20file.pdf*
Therefore, please add into method *MultipartFormDataOutput.addFormData(String key, Object entity, MediaType mediaType, String filename)* the parameter that will disable filename encoding
* Firefox client: Content-Disposition: form-data; name=""key""; filename=""La_R��gle_du_jeu""
* Jersey client: Content-Disposition: form-data; filename=""La_R��gle_du_jeu""; modification-date=""Thu, 07 Feb 2019 08:11:20 GMT""; size=4; name=""key""
* RESTEasy 3.0.24 client (WF11): Content-Disposition: form-data; name=""key""; filename=""La_R��gle_du_jeu""
* RESTEasy 3.6.1 client (WF14): Content-Disposition: form-data; name=""key""; filename*=utf-8''La_R%C3%A8gle_du_jeu

Default behaviour should be '{{filename=""non-encoded-characters""}}', we can consider to use some optional client parameters that allows to use ""%"" encoding. According to [chapter 2 of rfc7578|https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7578#section-2], we can consider to use something like 'filename=""La_R%C3%A8gle_du_jeu""' if this approach is required by some client (java) property (because of backward compatibility). In this case, RESTEASY-2046 issue needs to be considered as well.
BugClerk (automated tool) lives at this address: https://github.com/jboss-set/bug-clerk","29/May/19 10:30 AM;dcihak;Verified with EAP 7.2.2.CP.CR1.",,,,,,

Feature Request: Possibility to define default sub-domain for kafka cluster routes,ENTMQST-600,12779051,Task,Done,ENTMQST,AMQ Streams,software,scholzj,,,Major,Done,,cpandey,cpandey,12/Nov/18 8:16 PM,11/Mar/19 4:35 AM,29/Oct/19 12:21 PM,11/Mar/19 4:35 AM,1.0.0.OCP.GA,,1.1.0.GA,,openshift-integration,,,,0,enhancement,"- The default sub domain is set up with a certificate signed by a trusted CA. Customer manage these in a loadbalancer in front of Openshift, to limit the number of machines/clusters that need to be changed when customer get a new certificate. In Openshift we use the selfsigned certificate so the setup in the loadbalancer is tls reencrypt to this domain.This makes the tls handshake to the kafka cluster fail.

- Customer would like to have the possibility to setup a seperate subdomain for kafka clusters in openshift so that he can set the loadbalancer for routes on this subdomain to use passthrough and not reencrypt",OpenShift,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,2018-11-15 05:23:10.0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,ENTMQST-49,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,0.0,8.0,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"1|z03cm5:i",9223372036854775807,,,,0.0,,,,2019.3,,,1.0,,1.1.0.GA,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,,"15/Nov/18 5:23 AM;scholzj;The workaround for this is to manually edit the address in the Routes - the CO will take the address, run rolling update to change the broker configs and certificates and use the new manually set address. That should help everyone who needs this until this feature is implemented.","16/Nov/18 2:56 AM;cpandey;KCS:

The data migration is done while Debezium is down to make sure we don't run into the known limitation described at https://debezium.io/docs/connectors/postgresql/#streaming-changes
Our deployment process includes the following steps:

I cannot reproduce the issue. Could you please check theis page - https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/UserGuide/CHAP_PostgreSQL.html and let me know which commit of wal2json plugin is used for you database version.
Also I 'd really appreaciate if you could prepare an example sequence of DDL + DML statements that lead to the above described error. Important! It is necessary also to record TX boundaries.
Also could you please use {{pg_recvlogical}} to provide me with a sample of JSON replication message that is sent by postgres database?

Thanks a lot for your help","08/Mar/19 9:43 AM;wouter.bancken.aca;[~jpechanec]
I can already provide you the following information:
2. Example json (as logged by Kafka Connect):
This seems to indicate that the columnoptionals field is not included? At the time this event was created `certificate_id` was nullable.
3. For DDL and DML I'll try to produce a limited example
I think I found the cause for my issue.
* We recently did a Postgres update on Amazon which also bumped the commit of wal2json that is used.

* The old version used commit 645ab69 which did not support the ""include-not-null"" option yet which is responsible for introducing the columnoptionals field. * Our new version does support the option so the problem was probably in the replication slot for the connector that was created with the old postgres version and that was now reused.
This also explains why the issue could not be reproduced locally.
I think we can close this issue, my apologies for the interruption.
